private.name: ClearSpace

data.narrative:
Vyoma is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Stefan Frey and Luisa Buinhas, having raised €15M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 private companies at Series A and adjacent Series B stages within the same sector, anchored by comparable names including Aalyria, ClearSpace, Hubble Network, and Sift.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The four comparable companies span a valuation range of $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria), with a median of $400M. This method is appropriate given the alignment on sector and financing stage. The public-comp anchoring method, while relevant given the availability of five Space & Aerospace public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median P/S of 49.8x, cannot be applied due to the absence of Vyoma's revenue data—standard for private-company analysis.

The triangulated valuation range supported by available data is $340M to $460M, with a median point of $400M. This range reflects single-method confidence; the peer set includes both earlier-stage pure-plays like ClearSpace (at parity) and more advanced operators valued substantially higher. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to comparables, market segment (orbit/application-specific dynamics), and execution risk inherent to frontier aerospace. Additional financials would enable revenue-based validation.
